doctor_turn: Hello, I’m Dr. Lee. What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Uh, hi, Dr. Lee. So, um, I've been having this, like, worsening breathlessness for the past two months. It gets really bad when I walk more than, uh, 50 meters or when I climb stairs, like three or four flights. I’m kinda worried about it, to be honest.

doctor_turn: Can you describe where you feel the breathlessness the most?
patient_turn: Um, it’s mostly when I’m, like, trying to do anything physical, you know? Like walking or climbing stairs. But, honestly, it feels worse when I’m lying down. I just can’t catch my breath, and it’s really frustrating.

doctor_turn: What does the breathlessness feel like for you?
patient_turn: Oh, I was actually just thinking about how I need to call my sister later. She’s been asking about my cat, and I really need to get some groceries too.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the breathlessness starting?
patient_turn: Uh, I think it started about two months ago. At first, it was just a little bit, but then it got worse, you know? Now, I really struggle with it, especially when I’m lying flat.

doctor_turn: How does the breathlessness change when you’re doing different activities?
patient_turn: Well, um, it gets a lot worse when I’m doing anything physical, like walking or climbing stairs. I can usually manage, like, a little bit, but if I go over 50 meters or so, I really start to feel it. And, um, when I’m lying down, it’s just, like, really hard to breathe, so I have to use pillows to help.

doctor_turn: Can you describe where you feel the breathlessness the most?
patient_turn: Uh, it’s mostly in my chest, I guess? It feels tight, and I just can’t seem to get enough air, especially when I’m active or lying down. It’s, um, really uncomfortable and kind of scary, to be honest.

doctor_turn: When did you first notice the breathlessness starting?
patient_turn: Um, I think it was maybe a couple of months ago? Or perhaps a bit longer, I’m not really sure. It just kind of crept up on me, I guess.

doctor_turn: What medications are you currently taking?
patient_turn: Um, I’m taking aspirin, ramipril, and simvastatin. I’ve been on those for a while now, you know, to manage my heart issues, hypertension, and cholesterol. But, honestly, with this breathlessness, I’m not sure if they’re enough anymore.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any swelling in your legs or feet recently?
patient_turn: Yeah, actually, I have noticed some swelling in my legs. It started around the same time as the breathlessness, and, um, it’s been pretty bothersome. I didn’t really think much of it at first, but now it’s hard to ignore.

doctor_turn: Have you had any cough or noticed any sputum production?
patient_turn: Um, I don’t really have a cough, except for, like, an occasional non-productive one. But I have noticed some frothy sputum, which is, uh, kind of concerning. It’s not something I’ve experienced before, so it’s definitely adding to my worries.

doctor_turn: Have you had any recent infections or illnesses, like a cold or flu?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t had any recent infections or illnesses, like a cold or flu. I mean, I feel pretty healthy otherwise, except for this breathlessness and the leg swelling. So, um, I’m not sure what’s going on, to be honest.

doctor_turn: Have you traveled recently or had any surgeries aside from your stenting?
patient_turn: No, I haven’t traveled recently, and the only surgery I’ve had was the stenting four years ago. I haven’t had any other procedures or anything like that, so, um, I’m not really sure why I’m feeling this way now.

doctor_turn: Do you live alone or with someone?
patient_turn: I live alone in my apartment. It’s, um, kind of quiet, I guess. Sometimes I wish I had someone around, especially with how I’ve been feeling lately.

doctor_turn: What do you do for work?
patient_turn: I work as a cashier. It’s, um, not super physically demanding, but I’ve noticed that even that’s been getting harder with this breathlessness. I’m just, like, really tired all the time now.

doctor_turn: Have you ever smoked, or do you currently smoke?
patient_turn: I used to smoke for about 40 years, like, 10 cigarettes a day, but I don’t smoke anymore. I quit, um, a while back, but I guess I’m still worried about the effects it might have had on my health.

doctor_turn: Do you drink alcohol or use any recreational drugs?
patient_turn: No, I don’t drink alcohol or use any recreational drugs. I’ve just been focusing on managing my health, but, um, with everything going on, I’m kinda worried about what’s happening with my breathing.

doctor_turn: Is there any family history of heart disease or other related conditions?
patient_turn: Yeah, um, my father had a heart attack at age 60, so there’s definitely a family history of heart disease. I guess that’s part of why I’m so anxious about my own health right now, especially with these symptoms.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any increase in fatigue or a decrease in your activity levels recently?
patient_turn: Yeah, definitely. I’ve noticed a big increase in fatigue, and my activity levels have really dropped. I just don’t have the energy to do much anymore, and it’s, um, really frustrating. I used to be more active, but now I feel like I can barely keep up with daily things.

doctor_turn: How would you describe your diet lately?
patient_turn: Um, to be honest, my diet hasn’t been very well balanced lately. I’ve been, like, grabbing whatever’s easy, you know? I know I should be eating better, but with everything going on, it’s just been hard to focus on that.

doctor_turn: How has your activity level changed in the past couple of months?
patient_turn: Well, um, it’s really decreased a lot. I used to be more active, but now I find it hard to even do simple things, like walking or climbing stairs. I just get so out of breath, and it’s, like, really discouraging.

doctor_turn: Have you noticed any swelling in your legs or feet?
patient_turn: Yeah, I have noticed some swelling in my legs. It started around the same time as the breathlessness, and, um, it’s been pretty bothersome. I didn’t really think much of it at first, but now it’s hard to ignore.
